stories.headline: Thousands across New Zealand preparing to evacuate as Cyclone Vaianu closes in
stories.summary: Thousands of New Zealanders have been ordered to evacuate their homes as Cyclone Vaianu approaches the North Island. Authorities have warned of potential coastal flooding and severe weather conditions, prompting multiple states of emergency and mass evacuations. The subtropical system is expected to make landfall on the northeast coast, with the nation's weather forecaster warning of possible flooding in Auckland, the country's most populous city. Evacuation orders have been issued for several areas, with residents urged to prepare immediately. The cyclone's impact is anticipated to be significant, leading to urgent warnings and preparations across the region. New Zealand's North Island is bracing for the storm's arrival, with thousands of people preparing to evacuate as the situation continues to develop.

stories.headline: Watch: Dramatic splashdown as Artemis II astronauts return to Earth after historic journey around Moon
stories.summary: Artemis II astronauts have successfully splashed down in the Pacific Ocean, marking the end of their historic journey around the Moon. The crew's spacecraft re-entered Earth's atmosphere and landed near San Diego, completing the first lunar crewed mission in over half a century. NASA shared live footage of the splashdown on social media, announcing the successful completion of the mission. The astronauts' return to Earth was broadcast live, with the livestream beginning at 6:30 p.m. EDT and the splashdown occurring at 8:07 p.m. EDT. The Artemis II mission aimed to explore the Moon and pave the way for future lunar missions. The successful splashdown marks a major milestone in the mission and a significant achievement for NASA's space program. The crew's safe return to Earth brings an end to their long journey to the Moon and back.

stories.headline: ‘Strait of Hormuz will open, with or without Iran’: Donald Trump ahead of peace talks in Pakistan
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump stated that the Strait of Hormuz will reopen "fairly soon" with or without Iran's cooperation. Trump's comments came ahead of planned peace talks in Pakistan, where US negotiators will focus on preventing Tehran from obtaining a nuclear weapon. The US had previously presented a 15-point peace proposal to Iran through Pakistani officials. Trump asserted that the strait will open "with or without" Iran, and if it doesn't, the US will "be able to finish it off one way or the other." The statement suggests a firm stance by the US on the issue, as tensions between the US and Iran remain high. The peace talks in Pakistan aim to find a diplomatic solution to the conflict between the two nations.

stories.headline: California governor candidate Eric Swalwell denies sexual assault allegations
stories.summary: US Representative Eric Swalwell, a Democrat and leading candidate for California governor, has been accused of sexual assault by a former congressional staffer. The allegations, which Swalwell denies as false and politically motivated, were reported by the San Francisco Chronicle. A one-time aide claimed Swalwell sexually assaulted her twice when she was too intoxicated to consent. Following the reports, multiple staffers resigned from Swalwell's campaign, and prominent allies and rival candidates called on him to exit the race. Swalwell's campaign is reeling from the allegations, with some backers abandoning his gubernatorial bid. The accusations have sparked widespread criticism, with many demanding Swalwell quit the governor's race. Swalwell maintains the allegations are untrue and motivated by politics.

stories.headline: Tim Cook celebrates Artemis II return: ‘Taking iPhone photography to new heights’
stories.summary: Artemis II crew members were allowed to carry smartphones onboard, capturing stunning space photography. Apple CEO Tim Cook congratulated the astronauts, praising their inspiring mission and exceptional photography using an iPhone. The crew, consisting of Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen, took remarkable images of Earth and the Moon. Cook stated that the mission had successfully captured the wonders of space, and the iPhone photography had taken mobile photography to new heights. He commended the astronauts on social media platform X, noting that their work continues to inspire people globally. The Artemis II mission marked a unique opportunity for iPhone photography in space, with the crew's images showcasing the device's capabilities in a new and extreme environment. Cook's message highlighted the intersection of technology and space exploration.

stories.headline: NVIDIA’s DLSS 5 Demo Video Briefly Taken Down Because YouTube’s Take Down Process Sucks
stories.summary: NVIDIA's DLSS 5 demo video was briefly taken down from YouTube due to a copyright infringement claim. The video was not removed by NVIDIA, but was deactivated by YouTube after Italian TV station La7 filed a copyright claim. La7 had used footage from NVIDIA's announcement trailer in their own content, and then claimed copyright ownership, resulting in the takedown. The DLSS 5 announcement trailer is currently offline on YouTube, with other creators also affected by similar copyright claims. The removal is not related to NVIDIA retooling the technology, but rather a result of YouTube's automated copyright infringement process. The incident highlights issues with YouTube's takedown process, which can lead to false or misleading claims resulting in content being removed. NVIDIA's video is currently unavailable due to the claim by La7.

stories.headline: New collaboration targets muscle loss treatments for space missions
stories.summary: The Korea University College of Medicine's Center for Myokine Convergence Research and biotechnology firm MFC have signed a memorandum of understanding to develop treatments for muscle loss in astronauts. The collaboration aims to combat skeletal muscle deterioration caused by microgravity during long-duration space missions. The partnership, formalized on April 10, 2026, builds on a previous agreement and will focus on astronaut sarcopenia, rare muscle disorders, and pharmaceutical development. The goal is to accelerate the development of next-generation muscle disorder therapeutics, supporting long-duration space missions and potentially benefiting people experiencing age-related muscle loss on Earth. The collaboration will expand research into space environments to address the critical challenge of muscle loss in astronauts.

stories.headline: Microsoft Teams is finally fixing those awkward "can you hear me?" moments
stories.summary: Microsoft Teams is introducing a new feature to eliminate awkward "can you hear me?" moments during virtual meetings. This feature, already available on apps like Zoom, allows users to test their audio and video before joining a meeting. The update aims to reduce troubleshooting time and minimize disruptions. To ensure a smooth meeting experience, users can switch to a wired connection, clear cache, and conduct browser tests. A real-time mirror view will also be available, enabling users to verify their camera and microphone are working correctly. This update is part of Microsoft Teams' efforts to improve its platform for calls, meetings, and collaboration, making virtual meetings more efficient and productive. The new feature will help users avoid common issues and focus on their content, making virtual communication more seamless.

stories.headline: Rory McIlroy US Masters madness as Bryson DeChambeau crumbles Rory McIlroy delivered one of the most incredible finishes in major golf history with birdies on six of Friday’s last seven holes to seize a record six-stroke l
stories.summary: Rory McIlroy made a stunning comeback at the US Masters, birdying six of the last seven holes on Friday to take a record six-stroke lead. This followed a discouraging opening round where he shot an even-par 72 with two late double bogeys. McIlroy had sent a text to his psychologist, Dr. Bob Rotella, after the first round, but his confidence was restored with his impressive finish. Meanwhile, Bryson DeChambeau's chances crumbled after his second shot into the 11th hole landed in the pond. McIlroy, the defending champion, is seeking to win his seventh major title and has been backed to win six more majors. His remarkable turnaround has put him in a strong position to achieve this goal at the Masters, where he is making his 18th start. McIlroy won the 2025 Masters in a playoff over Justin Rose.

stories.headline: NBA: Knicks rout Raptors, wrap up East's No. 3 seed Jalen Brunson scored 14 of his game-high 29 points in the third quarter as the New York Knicks clinched the No. 3 seed in the Eastern Conference with a 112-95 v
stories.summary: Jalen Brunson scored 14 of his game-high 29 points in the third quarter as the New York Knicks clinched the No. 3 seed in the Eastern Conference. The Knicks defeated the visiting Toronto Raptors 112-95 on Friday. Brunson's performance helped secure the win, with the Knicks ultimately outscoring the Raptors by 17 points. The victory locked in the Knicks' position as the third seed in the Eastern Conference. The team's win was fueled by Brunson's strong third-quarter performance, where he scored 14 points to help the Knicks pull ahead. The final score was 112-95 in favor of the Knicks, solidifying their third-place standing in the conference.

stories.headline: Masters Round 2: What to know about Rory McIlroy’s history-making round History suggests McIlroy's six-stroke lead at the Masters is close to insurmountable.
stories.summary: Rory McIlroy has taken a commanding six-stroke lead at the Masters, setting a record 36-hole lead at Augusta. This is his 18th Masters start, and he achieved the lead after taking three weeks off prior to the tournament. McIlroy produced a strong finish in Round 2, recording a 7-under 65 and hitting six birdies on the last seven holes. He is now six strokes ahead of Patrick Reed and Sam Burns. Despite his significant lead, the dry and firm conditions at Augusta National and the caliber of his pursuers suggest there could still be drama to come. McIlroy's lead is considered close to insurmountable, with history suggesting it will be difficult for others to catch up. He will look to maintain his lead in the third round.

stories.headline: Boston Celtics tie NBA record with 29 3-pointers in win over Pelicans The Celtics’ 29 3-pointers on Friday equal their total from Oct. 22, 2024 against the New York Knicks.
stories.summary: The Boston Celtics tied a franchise and NBA record with 29 3-pointers in a win over the New Orleans Pelicans. This achievement equals their previous record set on October 22, 2024, against the New York Knicks. The Celtics secured the No. 2 seed in the Eastern Conference playoffs with this victory. By hitting 29 three-pointers, they blitzed the Pelicans at the 3-point line, achieving a significant win. This record-tying performance marks a notable milestone for the team, demonstrating their proficiency from beyond the arc. The Celtics' strong showing has positioned them well for the upcoming playoffs, solidifying their second seed in the East.

stories.headline: Kerala teen who went missing during family visit to Karnataka's Chikkamagaluru found dead
stories.summary: A 15-year-old girl from Kerala, Sreenanda, was found dead in a valley near Manikyadhara waterfalls in Karnataka's Chikkamagaluru district on Friday. She had gone missing during a family trip to the tourist site on Tuesday evening. Sreenanda, a class 10 student, was part of a 40-member trekking team. An extensive search effort by police and fire force personnel from both Palakkad and Chikkamagaluru led to the recovery of her body after a four-day search. The family had visited the hilly terrain of Chikkamagaluru, and her disappearance sparked a massive search operation. The circumstances surrounding her death are being investigated, with her family raising suspicions of kidnapping, although the exact cause of death has not been confirmed. The incident has prompted concerns about safety in tourist areas.

stories.headline: ‘Someone kidnapped and drugged her’: Missing Kerala girl found dead near Chikkamagaluru waterfall in Karnataka; kin make big claim
stories.summary: A 14-year-old girl from Kerala, Sreenanda, was found dead near a waterfall at Manikyadhara Hills in Chikkamagaluru, Karnataka. She had been reported missing since Tuesday while on a family trip to fulfill a vow and enjoy a holiday. Rescuers used a drone to locate her body, which was found in a nearly 2,000 feet deep gorge. Police suspect she may have accidentally slipped and fallen, but have not ruled out other possibilities. The girl's relatives claim she was kidnapped and drugged, stating that someone must have seen her alone and taken her away by force. The family had been searching for her since her disappearance, and her body was recovered three days later. The police are considering the possibility of a well-planned abduction, and an investigation is underway.

stories.headline: Expert Explains | The long, fractured history of Manipur, and the road ahead
stories.summary: Manipur's history is marked by fractures and conflicts, with a complex past influencing its present. The region has experienced numerous struggles, including ethnic tensions and demands for autonomy. Experts explain that understanding Manipur's history is crucial to navigating its future. The state's troubled past is characterized by insurgency, violence, and political unrest. To move forward, addressing the underlying issues and promoting dialogue is essential. Experts emphasize the need for a comprehensive approach to resolve the conflicts and bring stability to the region. By examining the historical context and current challenges, it is possible to chart a path towards reconciliation and development in Manipur. The road ahead requires careful consideration of the state's complex history and a commitment to finding solutions that address the needs of all stakeholders.

stories.headline: Grant Of Anti-Arbitration Injunctions Must Remain Confined To Exceptional Circumstances: CJI Surya Kant
stories.summary: Chief Justice of India Surya Kant stated that anti-arbitration injunctions must be confined to exceptional circumstances, exercised sparingly to preserve arbitral autonomy. He emphasized that courts should remain "passive" in arbitral proceedings unless there is manifest abuse. The CJI noted that such restraint is necessary to preserve the independence of tribunals while enabling courts to ensure procedural fairness. Anti-arbitration injunctions should be treated as exceptional measures, not the norm. This approach strengthens the credibility of arbitration and ensures its central role in resolving commercial disputes. The CJI cautioned against excessive court intervention, highlighting the need for a principled and sparing exercise of jurisdiction to maintain the balance between arbitral autonomy and judicial oversight.

stories.headline: [The Guardian] Reform’s petulance over slavery reparations shows it just doesn’t grasp Britain’s place in the modern world
stories.summary: Reform UK's home affairs spokesperson opposes financial compensation for slavery, stating the UK is "not an ATM for ethnic grievances." The party has threatened to block visa applications from countries, including Ghana, that demand slavery reparations. This move comes after Ghana and other countries called for reparations, leading to a potential visa restriction from the UK. A draft communique suggests re-examining slave trade reparations, adding pressure on the UK government. Reform UK's stance has sparked controversy, with the party's spokesperson saying "the bank is closed" on the issue of financial compensation for slavery. The UK's position on slavery reparations has become a point of contention, with some countries pushing for acknowledgment and compensation for historical injustices.

stories.headline: [Benzinga] Anthony Pompliano Says Bitcoin Has 'True Product-Market Fit'
stories.summary: Anthony Pompliano, founder and CEO of Pomp Investments, stated that Bitcoin is demonstrating "true product-market fit" due to rising interest from traditional finance and geopolitical actors. He discussed Bitcoin's market pullback and recent volatility, noting that the cryptocurrency experiences significant shocks every 18 months, similar to those seen in traditional markets once a generation. Pompliano pointed out that Bitcoin's volatility has decreased from 80 to 40, making the current drawdown the mildest on record. Institutional sentiment is mixed, with Bitcoin ETFs seeing $145 million in inflows but $272 million in outflows as prices fell below $64,000. Additionally, BlackRock transferred $170 million in Ethereum to Coinbase, sparking speculation about potential selling amid ETF redemption pressures. Pompliano's comments come amid ongoing debate about Bitcoin's price swings and market volatility.

stories.headline: Iran Reaches Ceasefire Agreement Proposing A $1 Billion Bitcoin Purchase
stories.summary: Iran has reached a ceasefire agreement with the US, proposing a $1 billion Bitcoin purchase. The two-week ceasefire aims to end hostilities between the countries. The agreement was made on the condition that Iran reopens the critical Strait of Hormuz. As a result, risk assets have risen, with Bitcoin's price surpassing $72,000 and US stock futures surging. Oil prices have collapsed following the confirmation of the ceasefire by Trump and Iran. The 45-day ceasefire plan is expected to come into effect, allowing for the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. The proposal is seen as a significant development in easing tensions between the US and Iran, with the Bitcoin purchase being a notable aspect of the agreement. The ceasefire agreement has led to a rebound in the crypto market and a surge in US stock futures.

stories.headline: Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) is increasingly becoming a tool in global geopolitics and financial markets
stories.summary: Bitcoin is becoming a significant tool in global geopolitics and financial markets. A recent development involves Iran proposing a ceasefire agreement that includes a $1 per barrel transit tax, which could be paid in Bitcoin. This move highlights the growing importance of cryptocurrency in international relations and financial transactions. Unlike traditional financial markets with fixed trading hours, Bitcoin markets operate 24/7, making it a unique asset in global trade. The use of Bitcoin in such agreements could impact its price and market trends. CoinGecko's live BTC/USD price chart and other cryptocurrency trackers are monitoring the situation, providing updates on Bitcoin's price, market capitalization, and trading volume. The development is part of a broader trend of cryptocurrency's increasing influence on global geopolitics and financial markets.

stories.headline: Markets gain 6% over the week, snap six week losing streak
stories.summary: The BSE's Sensex index rose 1.2% to 77,550.25 points on Friday, driven by gains in the banking and financial services sector. The Nifty 50 also closed 1.2% higher at 24,050.60. Except for the IT sector, all other sectors saw gains. The Sensex and Nifty ended the week nearly 6% higher, snapping a six-week losing streak and logging their biggest weekly gains since February 2021. This uptrend mirrors global market trends, as the Nasdaq, S&P 500, and Dow Jones Industrial Average also snapped their weekly losing streaks. The gains mark a significant turnaround, with the Indian markets posting their largest weekly gains in over five years. The surge in the banking and financial services sector led the market rebound, ending a prolonged period of decline.

stories.headline: Markets rebound 1 pc on buying in financial counters, rally in global peers; Sensex jumps 900 pts
stories.summary: Indian equity benchmarks Sensex and Nifty surged over 1% on Friday, driven by buying in banking and financial counters, as well as a rally in global peers. The Sensex jumped 900 points, with broad-based buying in banking, financial, auto, and realty counters. The rebound was also influenced by stronger global markets. Earlier, the Sensex had gained 650 points on strong buying interest in power, banking, and financial sectors. The Nifty and Sensex rebounded in early trade, driven by a rally in banking stocks and positive trending global markets. Key gainers included Infosys, RIL, and Maruti, with IT stocks also contributing to the surge amid hopes for an India-US trade deal. The Indian stock market rebounded sharply, driven by sustained buying from domestic institutional investors.

stories.headline: Stock indices end 6% higher after 6 weeks of decline due to US-Iran ceasefire, leading to a more risk-on approach from investors
stories.summary: US stock indices have ended 6% higher after six weeks of decline, following a ceasefire agreement between the US and Iran. The two-week ceasefire deal, announced last Wednesday, lifted investor sentiment and led to a more risk-on approach. Tech and Industrials sectors posted gains of almost 5%, while Small Caps rose 4%. The Dow Jones Industrial Average has climbed back into positive territory for the year, with US stocks notching a seven-day winning streak. However, there is disagreement between the US and Iran over whether Lebanon is part of the ceasefire deal. Despite this, the prospect of a prolonged end to hostilities in the Middle East has bolstered risk sentiment, prompting investors to buy stocks after weeks of uncertainty. The relief rally has marked a significant turnaround in the market.

stories.headline: Banking and financial services
stories.summary: Reuters.com provides the latest news and updates on financial services, including private credit, speculation markets, and mortgage payments. The website offers a daily roundup of market talks, covering various aspects of the financial industry. Additionally, it features FinTech news, focusing on digital payments, blockchain, neobanking, and artificial intelligence in finance. The editorial team updates the content daily, offering a range of resources, including podcasts, research, data analysis, and publications. The flagship ABABankingJournal and email bulletins provide in-depth coverage of the banking industry, including online banking services, electronic banking, loans, and overall industry trends. The platform aims to keep readers informed about breaking news and developments in the financial services sector.

stories.headline: Asia’s ‘panicked farmers’ brace for a looming rice crisis post-Iran war
stories.summary: The six-week war in Iran has disrupted global trade, causing concerns about food shortages, particularly in Asia. The near-closure of the Strait of Hormuz has blocked deliveries of fertiliser and fuel, affecting rice farmers. Soaring fuel and fertilizer prices are forcing many to abandon their rice fields, threatening food supplies. In Cambodia, where three-quarters of the population live in rural areas, panicked farmers are deciding whether to skip planting for the coming season. Some growers are reluctant to proceed without a guaranteed return, with around a tenth refusing to plant unless they can secure a fixed price. Harvest-ready rice fields are lying idle, and the situation is becoming unaffordable for many farmers, raising concerns about a looming rice crisis in the region. Rice farmers are struggling to cope with the increased costs, impacting their livelihoods.

stories.headline: Holidays at risk of cancellation as airlines face fuel crisis
stories.summary: Airlines are facing a fuel crisis, with European airports warning of a potential jet fuel shortage within three weeks. This shortage may lead to flight cancellations, disrupting summer holidays, including half-term breaks. Brindisi Airport in Italy has already depleted its fuel reserves, resulting in flight cancellations, and has advised airlines to refuel at other locations. Ryanair's CEO, Michael O'Leary, has warned that airlines may need to cut flights due to the shortage. The fuel crisis is attributed to the ongoing energy crisis, caused in part by the war in Iran. As a result, airlines are slashing routes, raising ticket prices, and facing fuel rationing. Interest in travel insurance has also spiked over the last month, as airline bosses warn of potential cancelled flights due to the jet fuel shortage. This crisis may impact holiday plans for many travelers.

stories.headline: Concern over medicine shortages caused by Iran war
stories.summary: The Iran war is causing concern over medicine shortages, with pharmacists calling for more transparency from Pharmac. Isosorbide mononitrate, an angina medication, is the first drug to experience shipping delays due to the conflict. Sir Jim Mackey, NHS England's chief executive, has expressed significant concern over potential medicine supply shortages in the UK, with some products at risk of running out within days. Despite this, a Government spokesperson claims there are currently no reported medicine shortages as a result of the conflict. However, the situation is being closely monitored for any impacts on the medical supply chain. The UK's NHS is reportedly "days away" from running out of some medical supplies due to the Iran war, highlighting the need for urgent attention to the issue. Pharmacists are seeking more information on the shortages and their causes.

stories.headline: Hong Kong’s listing reform 2.0: can it outshine global rivals for innovative firms?
stories.summary: Hong Kong's listing reform 2.0 aims to enhance the city's appeal for innovative firms, including AI and biotech companies, by slashing the market cap threshold. Since 2018, reforms have allowed pre-revenue biotech firms and companies with weighted voting rights to list. Clifford Chance has helped over a dozen innovative companies raise funds under the new regime. In 2025, Mainland Chinese listings on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange surged 153%, with several companies raising HK$6.99 billion ($900 million) in IPOs. As Western markets narrow, Hong Kong is becoming China's tech launchpad, standing strong against regional rivals like Singapore, Tokyo, and Shanghai. The city's economy faces ongoing challenges, but its stock exchange has seen a resurgent year for listings, solidifying its position in the competitive Asian financial landscape. Hong Kong's reforms have made it an attractive destination for innovative firms.

stories.headline: Sam Altman's home hit by Molotov cocktail, OpenAI CEO shares rare family photo in first reaction - ‘We try to be…’ San Francisco police arrested a 20-year-old man for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman's home in the city and for making threats outside the ar
stories.summary: San Francisco police arrested 20-year-old Alejandro Daniel Moreno-Gama for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's home. The incident occurred at 3:45am, but the device "bounced off the house and no one got hurt." Moreno-Gama was also accused of making threats outside OpenAI's headquarters. No injuries were reported. Altman shared a rare family photo in response to the incident, stating "we try to be..." The suspect was taken into custody, and an investigation is underway. The attack on Altman's home and the threats at OpenAI's headquarters have raised concerns about the safety of the company's executives and employees. The police have not released any further information about the motive behind the attack.

stories.headline: OpenAI CEO pens emotional post after a Molotov cocktail was thrown at his home, warning about the power of words and linking it to AI anxiety
stories.summary: A Molotov cocktail was thrown at the San Francisco home of OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, causing a fire to an external gate. The incident occurred around 4 a.m. Friday, and no one was injured. A 20-year-old man was arrested in connection with the attack, which also included threats to burn down OpenAI offices. The suspect allegedly threw the incendiary device at Altman's home, sparking a fire that was contained to the gate. San Francisco police made the arrest and are investigating the incident. Altman later wrote a post warning about the power of words and linking the attack to AI anxiety. The company and police confirmed that no one was hurt in the incident, which is being treated as a serious threat against the CEO and the company. An investigation is ongoing.

stories.headline: Former OpenAI Stargate Leaders Plan to Join Meta Platforms Three key players in OpenAI’s massive effort to set up hundreds of billions of dollars’ worth of artificial intelligence data center capacity are joining Meta P
stories.summary: Three key leaders from OpenAI's Stargate initiative are joining Meta Platforms, marking a significant shift in the company's artificial intelligence strategy. Peter Hoeschele, a critical player in OpenAI's effort to establish hundreds of billions of dollars' worth of AI data center capacity, is among the new hires. The move highlights Meta's prioritization of foundational hardware and data center orchestration to achieve artificial general intelligence (AGI). Meta has allocated $135 billion for 2026 to power its Superintelligence Labs, with the new recruits set to lead its compute unit and accelerate AI infrastructure expansion. The talent shift comes as OpenAI's Stargate project is being scaled back, with changes also expected in UK and Abilene data center plans. The recruitment of top architects from OpenAI's Stargate project is expected to drive Meta's AI compute ambitions forward.

stories.headline: Canada’s AI safety institute has now gained access to all of OpenAI’s “protocols,” Artificial Intelligence Minister Evan Solomon said Friday.
stories.summary: Canada's AI safety institute has gained access to all of OpenAI's protocols, according to Artificial Intelligence Minister Evan Solomon. This comes after a Canadian ChatGPT user was not reported by OpenAI, despite police saying the individual went on to kill eight people, including six at a secondary school. OpenAI has stated it is taking "immediate actions" to overhaul its safety protocols. Minister Solomon will meet with OpenAI's CEO, Sam Altman, to discuss how the company will strengthen its safety protocols and law enforcement coordination. The AI safety institute is working on a report and aims to ensure accountability. OpenAI's CEO has agreed to allow Canadian experts into the company's safety office to help evaluate future threats in the wake of the mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, B.C. The meeting is set to take place next week.
